ety: Prk. मढओ; S. मठ+कः; i.q. maḍhā, or maṛhā
def: s.m. A temporary building or shed (i.q. maṇḍuwā, or maṇḍwā, q.v.); an open shed or hall adorned with flowers (erected on festive occasions, as at a marriage, &c.)
more: s. m. See مڐها maḍhā, sign. 1.
subs: To roof in (or to erect) a nuptial hall, or bower
To climb the frame-work (a creeper, bel); — to ascend to the nuptial hall or bower (a bridegroom, &c.), to be married; — (fig.) to attain to perfection, to be perfected; — to succeed.

def: n. m. S. मण्डप 1. A temporary building or shed. 2. An open shed or hall adorned with flowers, erected on festive occasions, as at a marriage, etc. 3. Nuptial bower. Maṅḍhe tale ke suhele haiṅ . Prov. While yet endures the nuptial bower bright mirth and song shall rule the hour. 4. A marriage song. 5. A vinery. maṅḍhā chhavānā , v. a. To make the nuptial bower. Nīkā manḍhā chhavā, more bābal, parbat bāṅs maṅgāë! Marriage Song. bel maṅḍhe chaṛhnā , v. n. lit . to climb (a creeper). 1. To be married. 2. To succeed; to be perfected.

ety: maṅdhā˚ = māṅḍ, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि
def: v.t. (dialec.) To starch (i.q. māṅḍnā, and maṅḍiyānā).
s.m. (dialec.) A small round temple or cell.
more: s. m. A small round temple or cell. See منڐب manḍap.

ety: Prk. मढओ (with w inserted); S. मठ+कः
def: s.m. (rustic) = maṅḍhā, q.v.
part. adj. (f. āʼī), Starched.
s.f. = maṛhī, q.v. s.f. A small temple; — a cottage, a hut; a small shed; a jogī's hut or abode, a hermit's cell.
more: s. f. A jogīʼs hut or place of abode.

def: n. f. S. मण्डप A jogī's hut; a hermit's cell ( kuṭī ).
s. (dakh.) See مندر mandir.
s. (dakh.) See مندر mandir.
v.t. (dialec.) = maṅḍiyānā, q.v. v.t. To apply starch to, to starch; to dye (the hair, &c.).
v.t. (Dakh.) = māṅḍnā, q.v. v.t. To starch.
v.t. To cover, &c. (i.q. maṛhnā, q.v.); — v.n. To be held, be celebrated (in this sense prob. fr. S. maṇḍ).
v.n. = maṅḍnā, q.v. v.t. To starch.
v. a. मण्ड् to clothe. 1. To cover (a book with leather, or a drum with parchment). 2. To coat over; encase. 3. To throw on one; saddle one with. Unke hī sir maṅḍho! 4. To be held, celebrated.
s.f. The cost of having (a thing) covered (with leather), &c.
v. a. Caus. of مندَهنا manḍhvā'ī , n. f. The cost of covering anything with leather, etc.
v.t. To get or have (a book, or a drum, &c.) covered (with leather, or parchment); to cause to be encased, &c.
more: (causal of منڐهنا) v. a. To cause to cover (a book, drum, &c. with parchment).

def: s.f. Starch (made of rice-flour); paste ( = māṅḍ, q.v.); — (dialec.) the exudation obtained from the cocoa-nut tree; — (dialec.) the thigh; the knee.
s.f. A particular market for any one thing; a market, mart, an emporium; a shop or warehouse; a shop for the sale of grain, having a storehouse attached.
more: s. f. A market, a particular market for any one thing.

s. f. (dakh.) A shed or temporary building: see منڐهي manḍhī and مڐها maḍhā. ¶ munḍ-ī, s. (dakh.) Head; extremity, end: see منڐ munḍ. منڐي توڙنا munḍī toṛnā (i. q. منہہ پهيرلينا) To turn away the face.

def: n. f. A mart; an emporium.
more: n. f. A medicinal plant; Sphœranthus indicus , Wat.

def: Mandi Bahauddin (a city in Mandi Bahauddin district, Punjab, Pakistan)
wety: From Urdu منڈی (“market”), and named after Bahauddin, a Sufi saint.

def: s.m. A kind of fish.
s.f. A towel, napkin; — a turban, or a turban-cloth, woven with silk and gold thread.
more: n. f. H. mūṅḍ head. A turban woven with silk and gold thread.

s. f. 1. A tablecloth. 2. A towel. 3. A turband (high): or, a band ten or twelve cubits long, woven either partly with thread or silk and partly with gold, or wholly with gold thread, and applied over the turband. ( Qanoon-e-islam ).

def: s.m. A habitation, dwelling, house, mansion, palace; the dwelling of Hindū ascetics (as takiya is that of Muhammadan faqīrs); a temple, pagoda; — (fig.) the body; — a town; — the sea; — the back of the knee
more: s. m. A house, dwelling, a temple.
subs: s.m. A royal residence, a palace.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit मन्दिर (mandira).

def: s.f. A house, &c. (i.q. mandir); a stable; a bed; a mat used as a couch.
oblique plural of مَنْدِر (mandir, “temple”)
part. (f. -ā), Adorned, ornamented, decorated; overlaid (with gold, &c.); covered (with dust, &c.).
more: part. Ornamented, overlaid (with gold, &c.), covered (with dust).

ety: manḍiyā˚ = māṅḍī, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि (caus. aug.)+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To apply starch to, to starch; to dye (the hair, &c.).
v. a. S. मण्ड To starch ( kalap denā ).
s.m. (dialec.) Trunk (of the body).
more: n. m. The trunk (of the body).

def: v. n. To make a circuit; to hover round. 1. Chīleṅ manḍlā rahī haiṅ . 2. Ishq ke māre maṅḍlāyā phirtā hai . 3. Nīlṭāṅs jis sar manḍlāve mukaṭ-patī sūṅ lābhā pāve . Rus. Prov. The head round which a blue jay flits is marked for royal benefits.
n. m. A tumbler pigeon ( loṭan , 1).

ety: māṅḍ˚ = Prk. मड्ड(इ) = S. मर्द(ति), or मृद्ना(ति), rt. मृद्;+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To rub; to press down, to flatten; to tread or trample on, to crush; to tread out (corn), to thresh; to knead; — to stir up, excite, commit, perpetrate, make; to set on foot, set up, establish, institute; — to jot or note down, to enter, to commit to the books (an item, or a memorandum, &c.); to write (a letter); — to intend, determine.

v. a. S. मर्द्दन 1. To rub; tread or trample on. 2. To knead. Tum ant gaë, ham ant karāyo, māṅṛo chūn kutan ne khāyo . Rus. Prov. You went this way and I took that, the kneaded flour the dog got at. 3. To make; set on foot; institute. Khel māṅḍnā . 4. To write. Chiṭṭhī māṅḍnā . 5. To intend; determine.
v.t. To starch.
more: v. a. 1. ( मर्द्दन ) To rub, to tread or trample down. 2. To knead. 3. To make, stir, excite, commit, perpetrate, produce. 4. (from मंड ) To starch.

ety: manḍ˚ = Prk. मंड(इ) or मंडे(इ) = S. मण्डय(ति), caus. of rt. मण्ड्
def: v.t. To adorn, deck; to array.
v.n. To be rubbed; to be trampled on; to be kneaded (as dough); — to be busy or engaged; to be engrossed or absorbed (in, -meṅ); — v.t. = māṅḍnā, q.v.
s.m. (f. -ī), A frog; — the flower Bignonia indica.
s.m. (dialec.) A frog (i.q. meṅḍak, q.v.). s.m. (f. -ī), A frog; toad; — rump (of a horse)
subs: s.m. The frog abscess (a painful imposthume which grows on the sole of the foot, and usually terminates in the course of a few days by suppuration).
wety: From Sanskrit मण्डूक (maṇḍūka).

def: s.f. A frog; — the plant Siphonanthus indica, or Hydrocotyle asiatica (and several other plants).
s.f. A small frog.

ety: Prk. मंडवअं; S. मण्डप+कं, or Prk. मढओ (with w inserted); S. मठ+कः
def: s.m. A temporary building or shed; — an arbour, a bower, an alcove; — a shade or canopy of light framework.
s.m. A kind of grain, Eleusine coracana, or Cynosurus coracanus (i.q. maṛuʼā, or maṅṛwā).
more: s. m. An alcove, an arbour, a bower; a shed.

def: n. m. S. मडक A grain; Eleusine coracana , Wat.
s.m. = mandā, q.v.
s.f. = maṇḍwā, or maṇḍuwā, q.v. s.m. A kind of grain, Eleusine coracana, or Cynosurus coracanus (i.q. maṛuʼā, or maṅṛwā).
s.f. = maṅḍwā, q.v. s.m. A kind of grain, Eleusine coracana, or Cynosurus coracanus (i.q. maṛuʼā, or maṅṛwā).
s.m. Slowness, sluggishness; stupidity, dulness; torpor; apathy; weakness, languor; indisposition, illness; — inferiority, badness, vileness.
s.m. Forbidder, prohibiter, hinderer; — impediment, obstacle, opposition, bar, hindrance
more: n. m. See آرَ (6, 7, 8). 1. Bar. 2. Hindrance. 3. Prohibition. 4. Objection. 5. One who forbids or hinders. māne hukm . Arrest of judgment. māne h. , v. n. See bāz rakhnā , 5, 6. 1. To be a bar to ( roknā , 12, 3). 2. To prevent; prohibit ( barajnā , 1). 3. To object to ( eterāz k . 1).
(part. act. of منع Heb. ) s. m. 1. An obstacle, impediment. 2. A forbidder, preventer, hinderer. مانع هونا māniﻌ honā, To prevent, forbid, prohibit, hinder.
subs: s.m. Forbidder or hinderer of good
(-kā), To be a hinderer (of), to be a bar or obstacle (to); to prevent, prohibit, forbid; to hinder; to object (to)
What (is) the obstacle? what hinders? quid obstat?

def: s.m. corr. of منع manʻ, q.v. (cf. manā). s.f. Mother ( = mā, q.v.). s.m. (in Prosody) A molossus or metrical foot of three long syllables; — a magical or mystical formula; a name of Brahmā, of Vishṇu, and of Śiva.
s.m. pl. (of māněʻ, or of its fem. māněʻat), Hindrances, obstacles, impediments; interruptions.
s.f. Prohibition, hindrance, &c.
postpos. (dakh.) In.

ety: for A. منفعة, v.n. fr. نفع 'to be profitable,' &c.
def: s.f. Advantage; profit, gain; emolument; — use; useful purpose.
more: n. f. نفع gained. Profit; gain; emolument (فايده).
(from نفع) s. f. Emolument, profit, gain, advantage.

ety: n. of place fr. نفذ 'to penetrate, to pass through,' &c.
def: s.m. A place through which anything passes or penetrates; hole; passage; vent; outlet; a pass, a defile.

ety: pass. part. of نفي 'to deny; to reject,' &c.
def: part. adj. Negative; — rejected, repulsed; banished; — separated (from); — (in Alg.) minus.
more: (part. pass. of نفي) adj. 1. Negative. 2. Repulsed, rejected, banished.

def: s.f. A road, way, path ( = māg, or mārg); division; parting of (the hair), the line on the top of the head where the hair is parted; an ornament of pearls or of gold worn on the parting line of the hair
more: n. f. S. मार्ग The middle line where the hair is parted. māṅg bharnā , v. a. 1. To apply sendūr , etc. to the māṅg . Motiyoṅ se māṅg bharī rahe! Benediction. 2. To marry one. māṅg-bharī , Wom. n. f. 1. A feme covert . 2. A māṅg-paṭṭī , n. f. 1. Parting the hair in the middle. 2. Dressing the hair. māṅg paṭṭī meṅ lagā rahnā , v. n. To be engaged in dressing and ornamenting oneself. māṅg ṭīkā , Tir. An ornament worn over the māṅg. māṅg-jalī , Wom. n. f. A widow ( bidhwā ). Dil-jalī, māṅg-jalī, kokh-jalī hūṅ, banno, Kyā huā! mūṅh se nikaltā jo dhuāṅ rahtā hai . Wom. Jān Sāhab. māṅg chirnā , Slang., v. n. To be defloured. māṅg nikālnā, saṅvārnā, chīrnā, yā banānā , v. n. To part the hair in the middle.

n. f. S. मृग to seek, Pr. magganana . Want; demand, ( chāh , 21.). māṅg tāṅg-kar kām chalānā , v. n. To manage by borrowing from here and there. māṅg khulnā , v. n. To die (one's betrothed wife or husband). māṅg honā , v. n. To be in request or demand.

s. f. 1. A line on the top of the head where the hair is parted. 2. A betrothed damsel. 3. Prow. 4. Division. مانگ māng or مانگ پـٿي māng-paṭṭī (dakh.) A golden ornament worn over the line on the top of the head where the hair is parted, reaching to the back part of the head: Qanoon-e-islam, App. p. xviii. مانگ نکالنا māng nikālnā, To divide the hair in a straight line on the top of the head.
subs: (-se), 'To fill up the māṅg,' to apply vermillion (or red lead, or pearls, &c.) to the parting of the hair; to marry (a woman)

s.f. A woman with red-lead on the parting of the hair; a married woman, feme-covert; — a beloved wife; — māṅg-paṭṭī, s.f. The parting of the hair in the middle; dressing the hair; — māṅg-ṭīkā, s.m. An ornament worn along the parting of her hair (by a woman)
or māṅg saṅwārnā, or māṅg nikālnā, To form the parting of the hair, to part the hair in the middle.
wety: Deverbal from مانگنا (māṉgnā, “to request; ask; order”).

ety: conj. part. of māṅgnā; and = Prk. मग्गिअ = S. मृगयित्वा
def: part. Having asked, or begged, or borrowed, &c. (see māṅgnā)
subs: To carry on (or manage) a work or business by borrowing from here and there
v.t. To ask for and give (to), to borrow for (another)
or māṅg-ke, conj. part. = māṅg, q.v.
v.t. To ask for; to borrow.
wety: Deverbal from مانگنا (māṉgnā, “to request; ask; order”).

def: s.m. The head (of a boat).
v. a. To borrow for another, to ask for and give.
v. a. To borrow.
s.f. Want, request, demand; — betrothal; a betrothed damsel
subs: (-kī), A betrothal to become void; a betrothed wife (or husband) to die
(-kī), A demand to exist (for), to be in request or demand.
wety: Deverbal from مانگنا (māṉgnā, “to request; ask; order”).

ety: perf. part. of māṅgnā; and = Prk. मग्गिअओ = S. मार्गित+कः
def: part. (f. -ī), Asked for, demanded, &c. (see māṅgnā)
subs: part. f. Betrothed (a woman); — s.f. What is borrowed, a loan
v.t. To give on loan, to lend

ety: past conj. part. of mangānā, q.v.; — and = Prk. मग्गाविअ
def: part. Having sent for, &c.
subs: v.t. To procure and send; — to send for, &c. (i.q. maṅgānā, q.v.)
(-ko), To send for and give (to); to procure for
v.t. To ask for and bring; to procure (intens. of maṅgānā, q.v.).

def: To send for.
Borrowing.

s.m. Good luck, luckiness, good fortune, success; auspiciousness; prosperity; happiness, felicity, welfare, bliss; — a happy or auspicious event (as a marriage, &c.); a festival, festivity, rejoicing, revelry, pleasure, enjoyment; solemnity, any solemn ceremony or religious service; burnt-offering (on various occasions of rejoicing); — anything regarded as auspicious or tending to a lucky issue; a good omen; an auspicious prayer; blessing, benediction; — name of the planet Mars, or of its deified personification; — (for mangal-wār) 'Mars-day,' Tuesday; — (in Hindī poetry) a symbol for 8
more: n. m. 1. ( maṅglāchār ) Pleasure; enjoyment; revelry. 2. (A. mirrīkh .) The planet Mars. 3. Tuesday. maṅgal-chār, maṅglā-chār , n. m. 1. Festivity; rejoicing; congratulation. 2. A song of congratulation; an epithalamium. maṅgal-sūtr , n. A string of beads worn round the neck by married women, generally glass beads set in gold. Its absence is a sign of widowhood. (Hoshaṅgābād). Carnegy. maṅgal gānā , v. a. 1. To sing songs of joy. 1. Baiṭhī buṛhyā maṅgal gāve . Prov. 2. Gīt gavaniyāṅ sab saṅg mhāre maṅgal gātīāī rī, Ṭakā, potṛā, jachchā rānī, ham tere ghar lāī rī . Rus. Jachchā-gīrī . Upon our way we songstresses our songs of joy all sing, Our penny-gift and baby clouts, O lying-in-Queen, we bring! 2. To sing in one's praise or honor. maṅglā-chār k. , v. n. 1. To rejoice; revel. 2. To marry; join a couple.

s. m. 1. Welfare, happiness. 2. The planet Mars or its deified personification. 3. Tuesday. منگل چار mangal-chār, or منگلا چار mangalāchār ( मंगलाचार ) s. m. Festivity, rejoicing, congratulation, a song of congratulation, a marriage song or epithalamium.
subs: or maṅgalāćaran (˚la+āć˚), s.m. Benediction; prayer for the success of anything; pronouncing a blessing, wishing joy, congratulation; a song of congratulation; a marriage-song, epithalamium; an auspicious ceremony or observance; pious custom or usage; festive decoration; festivity, rejoicing; — worship or salutation to Ganeś at the commencement of an undertaking, or of a written work; (hence) preface, introduction
v.n. To make rejoicing, to rejoice, to revel; — to wish joy (to, -kā), to congratulate; — to sing a marriage-song; to join in marriage, to marry
s.m. The popular form of maṅga-lāćār, q.v.
(f. -ikā), or maṅgal-dāyī (f. -inī), adj. & s.m. Contributing to well-being, beneficial; — beneficent, benevolent
s.m. Anything (as flowers, fruits, and the like) which is offered as a good omen
s.m. & f. Good fortune; well-being, weal
s.m. Good tidings; tidings of the well-being (of); auspicious news; — the Gospel
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Proclaiming (or a proclaimer of) good news; felicitating; — a preacher of the Gospel (i.q. maṅgal-vādī, q.v.)

s.m. 'The lucky thread,' the marriage-string, a string tied by the bridegroom round the neck of the bride and worn as long as the husband lives; (local) a string of beads (generally glass beads set in gold) worn round the neck by a married woman (its absence is a sign of widowhood)
(f. -ikā), or maṅgal-kārī (f. -iṇī), adj. & s.m. Causing, or producing, welfare or prosperity; propitious; auspicious; beneficial; beneficent, benevolent; — a beneficent or benevolent person, &c.
s.m. A festive occasion; solemnity (as a marriage, &c.); an auspicious occasion

s.m. 'Pitcher of good omen'; a kind of vessel in which a light is placed, and which, at a wedding, is held on the head by women whose husbands are alive, and shown, as a good omen, to the bride, at the door of her house
s.m. Name of a town in India
s.f. A sort of carpet made at Maṅgalkoṭ
(-kā), To sing a song of joy, or of congratulation; — to sing in praise or honour (of)
s.m. An auspicious planet; a lucky star

adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Pronouncing a benediction; wishing joy; expressing congratulation, felicitating; proclaiming glad tidings; speaking to the benefit of others, or concerning their welfare; — one who pronounces a benediction, &c.; — a proclaimer of good tidings; — a preacher of the Gospel
(f. -ikā), s.m. One who imparts salutary instruction; — a preacher of the Gospel
vulg. maṅgalopkārak, adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Beneficial; — beneficent, benevolent; — a benevolent person, &c.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit मङ्गल (maṅgala).

def: s.f. A faithful wife; — an agreeable woman, &c.; — an epithet of Umā (wife of Śiva); — a species of bent grass, Panicum dactylon (syn. dūrva)
subs: s.f. The goddess Mangalā or Umā.

def: adj. & s.m. = maṅgal (used in comp.)
subs: s.m. & f. A musician, a singer, minstrel; bard; — a dancing-girl
s.m. The vehicle, or the abode, of well-being (often used as an epithet).

def: s. m. f. A musician or singer.
adj. & s.m. Rejoicing; triumphant; — born under the planet Mars
more: adj. Rejoicing; exhilarating. maṅglī , n. m. One born under Mars. Yeh laṛkī maṅglī hai .
adj. Triumphant, rejoicing. منگلي لوگ mangalī log, People employed on rejoicings.
subs: s.m. pl. People employed on occasions of rejoicing.

def: adj. (f. -ā), Auspicious, propitious, tending to good fortune, prosperous; indicating good fortune.
s. f. A sort of carpet made at Mangalkot.
s.m. An auspicious thing; — auspiciousness, prosperousness, prosperity, happiness, welfare; — an auspicious or festive ceremony, festivity; festival.
s.f. Asking; dunning; — a loan (i.q. maṅgnī); a debt.
more: s. (dakh.) Asking, to ask: see مانگنا māngnā.

ety: caus. of māṅgnā; — maṅgā˚ = maṅgāw˚ = Prk. मग्गावे(इ) = S. मार्ग+आपय(ति)
def: v.t. To send for; to ask for; to call for; to procure
more: v. a. To send or call for. ' Āth take ke geṅhūṅ mangāë, pīsegī ke nāh re?' 'Ṅāīṅ, mere bhore saiyāṅ, pīsā nāhīṅ jāë re.' 'Pīs poë ke āge rakkhā, khāëgī ke nāh re?' 'Hāṅ re, mere bhore saiyāṅ, khāūṅgī choṅ nāh re?' 'Here's six pence' corn I've brought, will you grind it or not?' 'Oh no, my simple spouse! I cannot grind a jot.' 'It's fore you ground and baked, eat it or not will you?' 'Why not, my simple spouse! I'll eat without ado.'
(causal of مانگنا q. v.) v. a. To send for, to ask for, to call for.
subs: &c., See s.v. maṅgā, conj. part. of maṅgānā.

def: adj. (f. -ī), 'Having strong joints,' well-knit, strong, powerful.
more: adj. Powerful. ¶ mangrā (Sans. मट्टक ) The ridge of a house.

def: adj. (f. -ī) = منگرا mangarā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), 'Having strong joints,' well-knit, strong, powerful.
s.f. (dialec.) The wrist; — the ankle; — pastern-joint (of a horse)
more: s. (dakh.) The wrist ( Harris ). منگٿ بند mangaṭ-band, A bracelet.

ety: prob. for māṅgat, and māṅgtā, imperf. part. of māṅgnā
def: s.m. A beggar, mendicant; a borrower.
s.f. A bracelet (i.q. mangaṭ-band).
s.f. Pulse meal made into a ball and fried (cf. next).
more: n. f. H. mūṅg . Balls of pulse ground and fried. mangauṛ'ī, magaurī , n. f. Dry pulse meal ball.

def: s.f. A ball of dry pulse-meal.
s.f. A ball of dry pulse-meal.
(f. -ī), a dialec. or rustic form of maṅgāyā, perf. part. of maṅgānā, q.v.
s. f. Name of a bird.
(fr. māṅgnā, q.v.), s.m. & f. One to whom a man or woman is betrothed; (commonly) a betrothed girl.
more: s. m. and f. One to whom a man or woman is betrothed.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit *मार्गयितृ (*mārgayitṛ).

def: s.m. The raised masonry round the mouth of a well.
s. m. The raised masonry round the mouth of a well.

def: part. & s.f. See s.v. māṅgā. part. (f. -ī), Asked for, demanded, &c. (see māṅgnā)

ety: māṅg˚ = Prk. मग्ग(इ) = S. मार्ग(ति), or मार्गय(ति), fr. मार्ग्, (rt. मृग्)
def: v.t. To ask for, request, demand, beg, solicit, entreat, pray, crave; to want, desire, seek; to borrow; — to ask in marriage; to betroth
more: v. a. 1. To ask; request; demand. Holī māṅge lākṛi, Divālī māṅge tel, Jorū māṅge kāpṛe, is meṅ paṛā jhamel . The Holī must have wood, the Feast of lamps its oil, The wife must have her clothes, there is no end to toil. 2. To solicit one's hand in marriage. Unkī laṛkī to māṅgī gaī . 3. To crave; beg; pray; solicit. Duā māṅgnā . 4. To borrow. Māṅge tāṅge kām nahīṅ chaltā. māṅge denā , v. a. To lend. māṅge-kī , adj. Borrowed. maṅget'ar , n. One who has been betrothed.
v. a. To ask for, to require, to demand, to beg, to pray, crave, solicit, to want, to desire, to seek, to betroth, to will. مانگ تانگ کرنا māng tāng karnā, To ask for.
subs: v.t. To ask for; to borrow.
wety: Urdu verb set مان٘گْنا (māṅgnā) مَن٘گْوَانا (maṅgvānā) Etymology tree Proto-Indo-Iranian *mr̥gás Sanskrit मृग (mṛga)vṛd. Sanskrit मार्ग (mārga) Sanskrit मार्ग॑ति (mā́rgati) Prakrit 𑀫𑀕𑁆𑀕𑀇 (maggaï) Urdu مانگنا Inherited from Prakrit 𑀫𑀕𑁆𑀕𑀇 (maggaï), from Sanskrit मार्ग॑ति (mā́rgati), from मार्ग (mārga), a vṛddhi derivative of मृग (mṛga), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *mr̥gás. Cognate with Assamese মাগা (maga), Be…

more: v. a. (dakh.) To ask for, &c. See مانگنا māngnā.
subs: (-se), 'To fill up the māṅg,' to apply vermillion (or red lead, or pearls, &c.) to the parting of the hair; to marry (a woman)

s.f. A woman with red-lead on the parting of the hair; a married woman, feme-covert; — a beloved wife; — māṅg-paṭṭī, s.f. The parting of the hair in the middle; dressing the hair; — māṅg-ṭīkā, s.m. An ornament worn along the parting of her hair (by a woman)
or māṅg saṅwārnā, or māṅg nikālnā, To form the parting of the hair, to part the hair in the middle.

def: s.f. Asking in marriage; betrothing, betrothal, &c. (see maṅgnī).

def: s.f. Asking in marriage; betrothing, betrothal; — a thing borrowed; a loan; — a thing obtained for the asking, or for nothing
more: n. f. 1. (E.) A loan. 1. Maṅgnī ke chandan ke na lagāī? Prov. Who would n't apply sandal (to his forehead) when he gets it for nothing? 2. Māṅge kī maṅgnī, guṛyā kā siṅgār . Prov. With remnauts begged from here and there the doll is dressed and decked out fair. 2. See rūkan . 3. ( Mah .) Betrothal ( sagāī ). Āj mere maṅgnī, kal mere biyāh, Parsoṅ lauṅḍyā o koī le jāe! Prov. Today she's betrothed, tomorrow she'll marry, The next day who wills may the girl home carry, maṅgnī denā , E. v. a. To lend ( māṅge denā ). maṅgnī k. , v. a. To betroth; affiance.
(see مانگنا) s. f. 1. Betrothing, asking in marriage. 2. A loan. منگني دينا mangnī denā, To lend.
subs: v.t. To take on loan, to borrow.
wety: From مانگنا (māṅgnā, “to ask for”).

s. m. Name of a month, the first of the Hindū year, according to some systems, or the eighth. See اگهن aghan, and مگسر magasir.

def: v.t. prop. ماننا mānnā, q.v.
more: v. a. To respect, regard, mind, attend to, observe, obey, heed, believe, accept, acknowledge, receive, experience, agree, allow, accede, assent, confess, consent, grant, admit, take, assume, trust, yield, submit, suppose, own, permit, to be set on.

ety: mān˚ = Prk. मान(इ), or माने(इ) = S. मानय(ति), caus. fr. rt. मन्;+nā = Prk. अनअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To respect, revere, esteem; to regard, heed, mind, attend to, observe, obey; to believe, trust, credit; to admit, allow, acknowledge, confess, own; to permit; to acknowledge the superiority of, to submit, yield; to agree to, assent to; to consent; to accept, receive; to take, assume, suppose; to take for granted, to grant; to hold, view, consider, regard as, deem, account; to hold to be true or right; to consider of importance; to approve; — to feel, experience, entertain; — to be set on
more: v. a. S. मानन. 1. To regard; mind; heed; attend to. 1. Nahīṅ māntā ? You wont mind? 2. Vahāṅ to jāvan kī bidh nā bane, yahāṅ rahūṅ to kab mānego jī? E voh pher bajī! Song. Holī . Go there I cannot anyhow, Stay here my heart will not allow: Ah! hark! he pipes again! 2. To respect; revere; esteem. 3. To believe; have faith in. 1. Yeh daī deotā kisī ko nahiṅ māntā . 2. Māno, deo; na māno, patthar . Prov. Believe and it's a god; believe not, it's a stone. 4. To accept; assent to; consent. Nautā mān līyā . 5. To acknowledge one's superiority. Ab woh bhī mān gaë . 6. To agree to; allow; accede to; admit. Mān na mān maiṅ terā mehmān! Prov. Like it or not, a guest in me you 've got! 7. To comply with; submit to; obey. 8. To yield; submit; own. Terī bāṅkī adā ko mān gaī maiṅ . 9. To take for granted; assume. Pahle yeh mān lo! 10. To observe; celebrate. Janam-ashṭmī kis roz mānoge ? mānne-jog , ad…
v. a. See مانا mānnā. برا ماننا burā mānnā, To take amiss, to take as an affront. بهلا ماننا bhalā mānnā, To take in good part.
subs: (intens.) To heed, mind, &c.; to acknowledge the superiority of; to submit, yield, come to terms with; to comply with; to acknowledge, confess, own, &c.
adj. Worthy of honour or veneration, venerable, &c.; worthy of acceptance or belief, credible, &c.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀫𑀡𑁆𑀡𑀤𑀺 (maṇṇadi), from Sanskrit मन्यते (mányate), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *mányatay, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *mányatay, from Proto-Indo-European *mn̥yo-, from *men- (“to think”).

ety: mān(nā)+at = Prk. अत्ती = S. आप्ति (āpi, caus. aug.+suff. ti)
def: s.f. Acknowledgment, assent; promise, vow; — desire, intention
more: n. f. H. mānnā to vow. A vow. Pālā kis kis tarah tumheṅ, jānī, Kaun mannat thī jo nahīṅ mānī? Wom. Shauq. My life! I reared you with such tender care, I made for you all vows that ever were. mannat baṛhānā , v. a. To take off the amulets when the vow has been fulfilled. Kyā din the, yāro! woh bhī the jab-ki bhole bhāle, Phirtī thī dāī le-kar, phirtī kabhī dadā le, Choṭī koī rakhā le, baddhī koī pinhā le, Haisli gale meṅ ḍāle, mannat koī baṛhā le, Mote hoṅ yā ki duble, gore hoṅ yā ki kāle, Kyā aish lūṭte haiṅ māsūm bhole bhāle! Nazīr. Such times, my friends, the days when we were young and innocent, Or carried by the maid, or in the nurse's arms content. Some votive cords, some top-knots for an offering meant, Or necklace round your neck, or vows with which your fate was blent, Or fat or lean, or fair or black, each state indifferent, What draughts of pleasure we in childhood drank incont…
subs: To fulfil a vow; to take off amulets, &c. (worn in consequence of a vow) when the vow is fulfilled
(-kī), To make a vow; — to observe or fulfil a vow.

adj. See s.v. manas. adj. Belonging to the mind or spirit, mental, spiritual; — s.m. Mind, the seat or faculty of reason and feeling; heart, soul; spirit; thought, wish, volition; — mānas, or mānas-sarowar, vulg. mānsarowar, mānsarwar, s.m. Name of a sacred lake and place of pilgrimage on Mount Kailās in the Himālaya mountains (the native place of the wild geese or swans; constant allusions are made to this lake in Hindī poetry, and the haṅs or rāj-haṅs (a kind of wild goose) is described as migrating to its shores every year at the breeding season)
subs: s.m. An epithet of the river Sarjū (of which lake Mānas is the source).
